input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets to `true` automatically. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (which your test projects already reference) sets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Library/production projects never get that property, so the conditional block is skipped for them.

**Important timing note:** `IsTestProject` is set by an SDK import, so it's already available in `Directory.Build.props`. If you ever hit an evaluation-order issue, you can move the block to `Directory.Build.targets` instead (same syntax), which runs after all package props are imported.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for a marker that distinguishes test projects — typically the presence of the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package reference or a naming convention.

The most robust approach is to use `IsTestProject`, which is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. Alternatively, you can key off the project name ending in `.Tests`.


I should provide a practical example showing how to structure the conditional in the props file.
